test_context: Strategy C — Phase 3 re-scored with Time Out EN+PT attempted (all 6 fetches FAILED — 404s or hub pages with no restaurant content); corrected both-pool rules applied; 5 original sources retained

sources.writer_profile: Miguel Pires is a Portuguese food critic and journalist who has covered Lisbon's restaurant scene for over a decade. He writes for Mesa Marcada, an independent Portuguese-language food publication with no commercial affiliations to any restaurant group. His editorial stance is critical and independent, focused on culinary craft and seasonal Portuguese cooking.

Culinary Backstreets is an independent food and travel publication founded on deep-dive neighbourhood reporting. Célia Pedroso is a Lisbon-based food writer with deep local knowledge; Austin Bush is a food journalist who also covers Lisbon extensively. Both contributors write independently with no commercial ties to Lisbon restaurant groups. The publication has no advertising relationships with featured restaurants.

Célia Pedroso is a Lisbon-based food writer who contributes to both The Guardian and Culinary Backstreets. Her Guardian article (April 2025) presents a personal top-10 selection of Lisbon restaurants with no disclosed commercial relationships. The Guardian is an independent UK media organisation; the article is classified as secondary due to its listicle format rather than long-form restaurant reporting.

Carolina Sobral is a food and lifestyle journalist at Observador, a leading Portuguese independent digital news outlet. Her September 2025 article covers new restaurant openings in Lisbon over the summer break. Observador has no commercial relationships with featured restaurants and maintains editorial independence as a subscription-funded news publication.

Daniela Sunde-Brown is a travel and food blogger who has lived in Lisbon and writes the Ola Daniela blog, focused on local restaurant recommendations for English-speaking visitors and expats. Her February 2026 guide is an extensively updated personal list of 42 restaurants across the city. She has no disclosed commercial relationships with featured restaurants.

restaurants.name: Canalha
restaurants.signature_dish: Open tortilla with shrimp and red peppers; grilled squid with sheep butter; raia alhada (stingray with garlic)
restaurants.quote: Chef João Rodrigues, known for Michelin-starred Feitoria, wanted a place that is transparent — no secrets in the kitchen — with a neighbourhood spirit rather than a tasca format (Culinary Backstreets). Fresh grilled squid with sheep butter (€25) and the open shrimp egg tortilla (€18) are pure joys — still the talk of Lisbon a year and a half after opening (The Guardian). You can walk in with €20 at lunch, or go all out with seafood and steak by weight (Ola Daniela).

restaurants.name: Pigmeu
restaurants.signature_dish: High-quality Alentejo pork dishes with seasonal vegetables; natural wines
restaurants.quote: Opened 2014, Pigmeu focuses on high-quality Alentejo pork, delicious vegetables and natural wines in a relaxed atmosphere (Culinary Backstreets). High quality Alentejo pork, delicious vegetables and natural wines — mains €10-17, standing as a model of honest nose-to-tail cooking (The Guardian).

restaurants.name: Lamina
restaurants.signature_dish: Arroz caldoso de rabo de boi (slow-cooked oxtail rice); cured and smoked tongue with herb sauce; beer bread with chicken liver paste and crispy chicken skin
restaurants.quote: A whole-product restaurant in the spirit of London's St. John — fire-cooked offal, aged meats and honest ingredients under executive chef Inês Azevedo, formerly of Hiša Franko

restaurants.name: Henrique Sá Pessoa
restaurants.signature_dish: Caldeirada with octopus; açorda with Norway lobster; escabeche with lily
restaurants.quote: Intimate 30-seat format at Páteo Bagatela with two counter seats overlooking an open kitchen; 450 reservations sold within three weeks of announcement, over 90% to Portuguese diners

restaurants.name: Yōso
restaurants.signature_dish: Omakase menu featuring Portuguese coast fish (sea bream, conger, tuna, squid), Hokkaido scallops with aged soy sauce and fresh wasabi
restaurants.quote: Chef Habner Gomes shows mastery of Japanese cuisine through relentless pursuit of quality Portuguese coastal products and exquisite kaiseki technique in a 22-seat space

restaurants.name: Parra Wine Bistro
restaurants.cuisine: Contemporary French bistro with wine focus
restaurants.signature_dish: Sea bass with clams and salicornia in beurre blanc; beef tartare with candied egg yolk and Cantabrian anchovy on toasted brioche
restaurants.quote: Sea bass with quality clams and classic butter sauce — rated 17/20 for kitchen; 400-reference wine list with 35-40% Portuguese selections curated by knowledgeable sommelier

restaurants.name: A Mesa do CAM
restaurants.signature_dish: Garden vegetables with fermented sauce; pork belly with Vinhais-style couscous and turnip greens; Armenian rice pudding tribute (Gatnabour)
restaurants.quote: Everything that comes from the farm is transported in electric cars — and then those same cars take back all of the organic material generated here, inside the Gulbenkian Museum's CAM

restaurants.name: Belmiro
restaurants.signature_dish: Partridge rice; hare with beans; wild pigeon rice; Ovos Belmiro (potato and egg omelette)
restaurants.quote: Chef Belmiro de Jesus: 'I like game — I was looking for something other restaurants didn't have.' Curated wine list built on 30 years of experience, distinct from what everyone else serves

restaurants.name: O Primo do Queijo
restaurants.cuisine: Portuguese petiscos, cheese and charcuterie, sandwiches
restaurants.signature_dish: Sandes de presunto com queijo (ham and cheese sandwich); cheese boards with 10+ daily varieties; smoked salt cod with olive oil and pickled beetroot
restaurants.quote: Owners changed the cheese and bread five times before settling — all products Portuguese except Coca-Cola. Conceived as an aperitif stop, not a full meal: 'eat a cheese board, then go have dinner'

restaurants.name: Sabores de Goa
restaurants.signature_dish: Goan curries; authentic Goan dishes
restaurants.quote: Owner Sérgio: 'This is a real Goan restaurant — other places serve Indian food.' Located on Rua do Forno do Tijolo alongside French bakers, Macanese dishes and coffee roasters

restaurants.name: Patuá
restaurants.signature_dish: Macanese cuisine
restaurants.quote: One of the few Macanese restaurants in Lisbon, on Rua do Forno do Tijolo — a street that captures the city's post-colonial culinary diversity alongside Goan, Brazilian and French options

restaurants.name: Juliana Penteado Pastry
restaurants.signature_dish: Pastries with lavender, geranium, orange blossom, bergamot, ginger, cardamom and pink peppercorn infused oils
restaurants.quote: The oils — lavender, geranium, orange blossom, bergamot, ginger, cardamom, pink peppercorns — add an intense punch of flavor that very few people use in baking, says Brazilian chef Juliana Penteado

restaurants.name: Bichomau
restaurants.signature_dish: Seasonal produce-driven dishes
restaurants.quote: Owner-cook Tomás Rocha: 'My thing is product.' Opened 2019 as a modern neighbourhood bistro distinct from the wave of new wine bars across the city

restaurants.name: Tati
restaurants.cuisine: Wine bar, organic and natural wines with light food
restaurants.signature_dish: Natural and organic wines; live music evenings
restaurants.quote: Founded in 2011 by Ramón Ibáñez (Barcelona transplant) in an 18th-century building behind Mercado da Ribeira — organic natural wines and live music in a historic interior

restaurants.name: Taberna Albricoque
restaurants.signature_dish: Octopus with sweet potato; razor-clam soup; seasonal Algarve produce
restaurants.quote: Seasonal, constantly changing menu features dishes such as octopus with sweet potato and razor-clam soup — chef Bertílio Gomes's Algarve-focused cooking in a renovated Santa Apolónia tavern

restaurants.name: Batata Doce
restaurants.signature_dish: Moamba (Angolan chicken stew with okra); moqueca de camarão (Brazilian prawn stew)
restaurants.quote: Delicious dishes including moamba, an Angolan chicken stew with okra, and moqueca de camarão — celebrating Lusophone post-colonial cooking at around €15 per main

restaurants.name: Zuari
restaurants.signature_dish: Crispy Goan samosas; bebinca (multilayered Goan cake made by daughter Débora)
restaurants.quote: Incredible crispy samosas and daughter Débora's traditional bebinca — a multilayered Goan cake — at around €14-15 per main in a family-run Lapa restaurant

restaurants.name: Mãe – Cozinha com Amor
restaurants.signature_dish: Fish and prawn rice; duck rice (€18)
restaurants.quote: Fish and prawn rice and duck rice (€18) are a contemporary take on a Portuguese staple — comfort cooking with love, in Estefânia

restaurants.name: Prado
restaurants.signature_dish: Sweet potato snack with mussels and curry; marinated mackerel with green apple and olive
restaurants.quote: Sweet potato snack with mussels and curry, and marinated mackerel with green apple and olive — creative seasonal plates at Michelin-recommended Baixa restaurant, mains around €16

restaurants.name: Garum Fish & Wine Bar
restaurants.signature_dish: Seabass rice (€16); carob petit gateau with fig ice cream
restaurants.quote: Seabass rice (€16) is the bestseller — and don't miss the dessert: carob petit gateau with fig ice cream, at a Japanese-inflected wine and seafood bar in Graça

restaurants.name: Petisco Saloio
restaurants.signature_dish: Bitoque (steak with sauce, chips and rice, topped with a fried egg); petiscos
restaurants.quote: Dishes such as the bitoque — steak with sauce, chips and rice, topped with a fried egg — at €8-14, addressing the closure trend among traditional Lisbon tascas

restaurants.name: Taberna (Bairro do Avillez)
restaurants.signature_dish: Steak and mustard sandwich (€15.75); broad-bean salad with chorizo (€10.50)
restaurants.quote: Steak and mustard sandwich (€15.75) and a more elaborate broad-bean salad with chorizo (€10.50) — José Avillez's casual taberna at the entrance of Bairro do Avillez in Chiado

restaurants.name: Café des Épices
restaurants.signature_dish: Tagine (chakchouka €14; almôndegas €16; frango €18; vaca €20; polvo €20)
restaurants.quote: Rugs covering walls and large chandeliers hanging from the ceiling — a Moroccan restaurant in Marvila with chef Soumia's signature tagines and a vibrant North African interior

restaurants.name: Quack Shack
restaurants.signature_dish: Quack burger (€9.90); Quack Signature (€12.90) — 140g duck meat patty with garlic butter and parsley
restaurants.quote: Michelin-starred chef Rodrigo Castelo opened this duck-only burger spot in Alvalade — 140g duck meat patty with garlic butter and parsley, Quack at €9.90 and Quack Signature at €12.90

restaurants.name: Broto
restaurants.signature_dish: Portuguese-inspired seasonal dishes rooted in family and affective memory
restaurants.quote: Chef Pedro Pena Bastos: 'I want to explore free Portuguese cooking — make it more accessible.' A return to casual dining from a former Michelin-starred chef, opening in late September 2025

restaurants.name: Barbela
restaurants.signature_dish: Aged fish and seafood preparations
restaurants.quote: Chef Leandro Carreira bringing to Santos 'an offering that people say Lisbon didn't have' — aged fish and seafood, a concept previously associated with his London work

restaurants.name: Maída
restaurants.signature_dish: Hummus (€7); artichoke and spinach (€10.20); shared mezze format
restaurants.quote: New Lebanese-Mediterranean house on Rua da Boavista in Cais do Sodré — concept built around communal mezze at the centre of the table, hummus at €7 and artichoke and spinach at €10.20

restaurants.name: JNcQUOI Fish
restaurants.signature_dish: Peixe e marisco (fish and shellfish) tasting menus
restaurants.quote: Chef Filipe Carvalho's fish and seafood restaurant on Avenida da Liberdade, opening October 8 2025 — later awarded Michelin Guide 'Opening of the Year' distinction for 2026

restaurants.name: Ultimo Porto
restaurants.signature_dish: Grilled whole fish; fresh seafood
restaurants.quote: Waterfront restaurant jammed against the docks with giant trucks carting shipping containers whizzing past the terrace — best grilled fish in Lisbon, lunch only

restaurants.name: O Velho Eurico
restaurants.signature_dish: Octopus lagareiro; braised meats
restaurants.quote: One of Lisbon's toughest reservations — a casual restaurant that helped kick off the modern tasca craze when it opened in 2019, with octopus lagareiro as the signature dish

restaurants.name: Taberna Sal Grosso
restaurants.signature_dish: Braised pork cheeks; tuna pica-pau; seasonal petiscos from hand-scrawled chalkboard menu
restaurants.quote: Intimate space with hand-scrawled chalkboard menus and classic dishes with contemporary flair — books months in advance; braised pork cheeks and tuna pica-pau are standouts

restaurants.name: Taberna da Rua das Flores
restaurants.signature_dish: Inventive petiscos rooted in traditional Portuguese recipes
restaurants.quote: Inventive Portuguese cuisine rooted in tradition — a favourite of locals and travellers alike for petiscos with contemporary flair in a narrow Chiado side street

restaurants.name: Cervejaria Ramiro
restaurants.signature_dish: Gooseneck barnacles; spider crabs; garlic prawns; fresh shellfish
restaurants.quote: The most famous Lisbon seafood restaurant — prehistoric-looking gooseneck barnacles, spider crabs and other exceptional fresh shellfish in a bustling environment. Not a quiet date night

restaurants.name: BouBou's
restaurants.signature_dish: Tasting menu: Bloom menu mixing flowers, sea and refined technique
restaurants.quote: Relaxed fine dining with tasting menu by French Top Chef winner Louise Bourrat — plant-filled room with Bloom menu combining flowers, sea ingredients and refined French-influenced technique

restaurants.name: Rosetta's
restaurants.signature_dish: Grilled peaches with burrata; zucchini fritters with dill; clams with beurre blanc and smoked ham
restaurants.quote: Every dish — grilled peaches with burrata, zucchini fritters with dill, clams with beurre blanc and smoked ham — was a 10/10. Tiny produce-driven share-plate restaurant in Bairro Alto

restaurants.name: Solar dos Nunes
restaurants.signature_dish: Alentejo regional cuisine; large portions of traditional dishes
restaurants.quote: Traditional Alentejo cuisine in Alcântara — a classic Portuguese restaurant with generous portions and authentic regional recipes in a straightforward no-frills setting
